title: Fresh Crunchy Greek Bacon Skewers
description: A paleo-friendly twist on traditional Greek cuisine, featuring fresh, crunchy, and comforting streaky bacon skewers, cured with Japanese-inspired shio-koji.
instructions: Cut the streaky bacon into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the shio-koji, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Add the bacon to the shio-koji mixture and marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated bacon, fresh parsley, and fresh oregano onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the skewers with additional olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the skewers for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the bacon is crispy and golden brown.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional fresh parsley and lemon wedges.
To assemble the skewers, start by threading a piece of bacon onto the skewer, followed by a piece of parsley and a piece of oregano.
Continue threading the ingredients onto the skewer, leaving a small space between each piece.
Once the skewers are assembled, brush them with the shio-koji mixture and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the skewers for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the bacon is crispy and golden brown.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional fresh parsley and lemon wedges.
